name: linux_proc_extras
explanation: The check reads files under the Agent host's `procfs_path` (defaults to `/proc`): `sys/fs/inode-nr`, `stat`, `sys/kernel/random/entropy_avail`, `<pid>/status`, and optionally `interrupts`. There is no required configuration field, no host/port/URL/credential, and no upstream service to probe. Discovery is a host-local presence check — "is /proc available?" — which is true on every Linux host. The only optional knob (`include_interrupt_metrics`) is a cardinality toggle, not an endpoint selector. Auto-config can therefore deploy the integration unconditionally on Linux hosts with its default empty instance. Direct analogue of btrfs (pattern: host-local generic, no network probe, no binary subprocess).
notes: Method `other` because there is no network probe — discovery is OS presence (Linux + readable /proc). No required fields and no per-host variation in endpoint.
